title: Review and setlist: Courtney Barnett shows that being a ‘creature of habit’ isn’t always a bad thing
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Courtney Barnett performed at Boston's Roadrunner, showcasing her new album "Creature of Habit" on May 13.
Barnett balanced new songs and fan favorites, performing tracks like "Avant Gardener" and "Depreston."
Opening acts included Brooklyn indie band Momma and folk rock musician Truman Sinclair.

title: MassGOP-backed lieutenant governor candidate fails to collect enough signatures to make ballot
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anne Brensley, GOP-endorsed lieutenant governor candidate, failed to collect 10,000 signatures needed to appear on the ballot.
Brensley's campaign blames forgery concerns, with Town Clerks reporting fake signatures to the Massachusetts Secretary of State.
Brensley plans to run as a write-in candidate if unable to qualify for the ballot through additional signature collection.

title: 7 takeaways from Patriots’ 2026 schedule
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots face eight 2025 playoff teams, including a Super Bowl rematch against the Seahawks in Week 1.
New England will travel to Germany for a game against the Detroit Lions on November 15.
The schedule includes six nationally televised games and a total of 27,980 travel miles for the season.

title: Here’s your chance to design a new Boston Public Library card
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston Public Library is hosting a Library Card Design Contest from May 15 to June 15.
Artists in three age groups can submit designs reflecting what the library means to them and their communities.
Winning designs will be featured on Boston Public Library cards for Library Card Sign-Up Month in September.

title: ‘This was my baby’: Blaze destroys popular Belmont ice cream shop, causes floor to collapse
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A three-alarm fire destroyed Moozy's Ice Cream Emporium in Belmont, causing a floor collapse.
Fire officials said a natural gas line rupture caused a secondary fire at the scene.
The cause of the fire is under investigation; Moozy's opened in 2012 and was a community staple.

title: Patriots officially unveil 2026 schedule that begins with Super Bowl rematch
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots will open their 2026 season with a Super Bowl rematch against the Seahawks in Seattle on September 9.
The Patriots' 2026 schedule includes a game against the Detroit Lions in Munich, Germany on November 15.
New England is set for six nationally televised games, including matchups on Sunday, Monday, and Thursday Night Football.

title: Did my vehicle miss out on an important safety recall?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2009 VW Jetta owner did not receive a recall notice for an ABS module software update issued in 2017.
Recalls may not affect every vehicle in a model year due to specific production criteria or factory limitations.
Owners can seek reimbursement from Volkswagen or NHTSA if repairs align with recall conditions not initially communicated.

title: Bruins assistant GM leaving organization to ‘pursue other opportunities’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Assistant GM Jamie Langenbrunner is leaving the Boston Bruins after 11 years to pursue other NHL opportunities.
Langenbrunner joined the Bruins in 2015 as a development coach and became assistant GM in the 2022-23 season.
Potential replacements for Langenbrunner include Adam McQuaid and Zdeno Chara, with McQuaid now director of player development.

title: What Sam Kennedy said of Jason Varitek’s future with the Red Sox
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox president Sam Kennedy expressed optimism about Jason Varitek's future within the organization despite his recent reassignment.
Jason Varitek was retained by the Red Sox after firing Alex Cora, though his new role has yet to be determined.
Reports suggest Varitek may not continue with the Red Sox, though he has not publicly commented on his plans.

title: Former Celtics star Isaiah Thomas hired by Boston as a pro, college scout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Isaiah Thomas, 37, hired by Celtics as a pro and college scout based in Seattle.
Thomas joins Celtics executives at the NBA Combine to interview prospects and learn evaluation processes.
Thomas played for the Celtics from 2015-2017, becoming a fan favorite and earning two All-Star selections.
